source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, it is known that $BA=BC$, $\angle B=120^\circ$, and the perpendicular bisector $DE$ of $AB$ intersects $AC$ at point D. If $AC=6\,cm$, what is the length of $AD$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Connect $BD$,\\
since the perpendicular bisector $DE$ of $AB$ intersects $AC$ at point D,\\
it follows that $AD=BD$,\\
hence $\angle A=\angle ABD$,\\
since $BA=BC$, $\angle B=120^\circ$,\\
it follows $\angle A=\angle C=\frac{1}{2}\left(180^\circ-120^\circ\right)=30^\circ$,\\
thus $\angle ABD=30^\circ$,\\
hence $\angle CBD=90^\circ$,\\
thus $CD=2BD$,\\
hence $CD=2AD$,\\
thus $AC=AD+CD=AD+2AD=3AD$,\\
further, since $AC=6\text{cm}$,\\
it follows $AD=\boxed{2\text{cm}}$.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'As shown in the figure'
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false in the input fields
the problem uses diagram-specific geometry entities and relations (triangle ABC, perpendicular bisector DE, intersection point D on AC), which are naturally grounded by the figure
although many relations are also stated in text, the labeling and setup still appear image-linked rather than purely textual
multi_step: the provided answer_text shows a chain of several deductions: AD=BD from perpendicular bisector, base angles A and C from BA=BC and angle B=120°, angle-chasing to get angle CBD=90°, then CD=2BD, then AC=AD+CD
solving requires combining multiple geometric facts rather than a single direct formula
this is a constructed-geometry problem with relational reasoning across points, angles, and segment lengths
verifiability: the target output is a single numeric length: AD=2 cm
the answer_text provides an explicit derivation that can be step-checked against standard geometry rules
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true support downstream checking feasibility
the conclusion can be verified from the stated conditions by independent geometric derivation
